title: Anti-CSRF Token Flow
description: Generation and validation of CSRF tokens via AntiCSRFService to protect state-mutating requests
token_generation_flow: START: Client needs to make a state-mutating request
GUI sends GET /get-anticsrf-token with Authorization: Bearer <auth_token>
Backend checks subdomain — BLOCK if subdomain is 'api' (relaxed CORS policy) → 404
Backend checks req.user authenticated → BLOCK if not → 403
[Decision] Does session queue exist in map_session_to_tokens?
→ NO: create new CircularQueue(10) and store in map
tokens.push(token) — if queue full, oldest token is evicted (circular)
Client attaches token as anti_csrf field in request body
token_validation_flow: START: State-mutating request arrives at protected route
Look up CircularQueue for session in map_session_to_tokens
→ YES: null out slot and return true (token consumed, one-time use)
→ YES: call next() — proceed to route handler
END: Route handler executes (logout / account deletion / session revoke)
token_expiry_logic: On push when full: oldest token is overwritten (evicted)
Test confirms: of 30 generated tokens, only last 10 are valid
Tokens are single-use: maybe_consume nulls the slot after first valid use
activity_decisions: Is subdomain 'api'? → YES: block (404); NO: continue
Is user authenticated? → NO: block (403); YES: continue
Does session token queue exist? → NO: create queue; YES: reuse
Is anti_csrf present in request body? → NO: error; YES: continue
Does consume_token return true? → NO: 400 error; YES: next()
key_relationships: Backend AntiCSRFService composes CircularQueue (one per session, capacity 10)
Backend AntiCSRFService registers route GET /get-anticsrf-token via eggspress
anticsrf middleware depends_on AntiCSRFService (via service registry 'anti-csrf')
logout.js, down.js, revoke-session.js all call consume_token inline
GUI AntiCSRFService extends Service (frontend), fetches token from backend endpoint
protected_routes: Any route using anticsrf() middleware factory
token_format: 32 random bytes as hex string (64 chars)
session_key: req.user.uuid (user UUID used as session identifier)
important_notes: Token endpoint blocked on 'api' subdomain (relaxed CORS) to prevent cross-origin token fetch
CircularQueue evicts oldest token when capacity exceeded — prevents unbounded memory growth
GUI AntiCSRFService fetches a fresh token before each protected request
